ucs.title: [LLM] Moses Staff DCSrv wiper service install (DiskCryptor-fork driver)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature destructive payload of Moses Staff (DEV-0500 / Marigold Sandstorm): the DCSrv kernel-mode driver — a fork of the open-source DiskCryptor — installed as a Windows service to encrypt the MBR with no ransom demand. The service name (DCSrv/DCrypt) and driver filename (dcsrv.sys/dcrypt.sys) are unique fingerprints documented by Check Point and Microsoft on Moses Staff intrusions against Israeli targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.process_name="sc.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*" AND (Processes.process="*DCSrv*" OR Processes.process="*DCrypt*" OR Processes.process="*dcsrv.sys*" OR Processes.process="*dcrypt.sys*") BY host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem WHERE Endpoint.Filesystem.file_path="*\\System32\\drivers\\*" AND (Endpoint.Filesystem.file_name="dcsrv.sys" OR Endpoint.Filesystem.file_name="dcrypt.sys" OR Endpoint.Filesystem.file_name="dcrypt_setup.sys") BY host Endpoint.Filesystem.file_name Endpoint.Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Endpoint.Filesystem")` ] | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Registry WHERE Endpoint.Registry.registry_path="*\\Services\\DCSrv*" OR Endpoint.Registry.registry_path="*\\Services\\DCrypt*" BY host Endpoint.Registry.registry_path Endpoint.Registry.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Endpoint.Registry")` ] | `convert_timeformat(firstTime,lastTime)` | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let _wiper_drivers = dynamic(["dcsrv.sys","dcrypt.sys","dcrypt_setup.sys"]);
let _wiper_service_tokens = dynamic(["DCSrv","DCrypt","dcsrv.sys","dcrypt.sys"]);
let driver_drops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\System32\drivers\", @"\SysWOW64\drivers\")
    | where FileName in~ (_wiper_drivers)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Account = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Signal = "DriverDropped",
              Artifact = strcat(FolderPath, FileName),
              ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256;
let svc_creates = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "sc.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "create"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_wiper_service_tokens)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Account = AccountName,
              Signal = "ScCreateService",
              Artifact = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256;
let svc_keys = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryKeyCreated","RegistryValueSet")
    | where RegistryKey matches regex @"(?i)\\Services\\(DCSrv|DCrypt)(\\|$)"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Account = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Signal = "RegistryServiceKey",
              Artifact = strcat(RegistryKey, " -> ", RegistryValueData),
              ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256 = "";
union driver_drops, svc_creates, svc_keys
| where Account !endswith "$"
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _wiper_drivers = dynamic(["dcsrv.sys","dcrypt.sys","dcrypt_setup.sys"]);
let _wiper_service_tokens = dynamic(["DCSrv","DCrypt","dcsrv.sys","dcrypt.sys"]);
let sc_create = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\sc.exe"
    | where CommandLine has "create"
    | where CommandLine has_any (_wiper_service_tokens)
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Signal = "ScCreateService",
              Artifact = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let svc_install = Event
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where Source =~ "Service Control Manager" and EventID == 7045
    | extend ServiceName = tostring(extract(@"(?i)Service Name:\s*([^\r\n]+)", 1, RenderedDescription)),
             ImagePath  = tostring(extract(@"(?i)Service File Name:\s*([^\r\n]+)", 1, RenderedDescription))
    | where ServiceName has_any ("DCSrv","DCrypt") or ImagePath has_any (_wiper_drivers)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account = "SYSTEM",
              Signal = "ServiceInstalled",
              Artifact = strcat(ServiceName, " => ", ImagePath),
              ParentProcessName = "services.exe";
let driver_drops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\System32\drivers\", @"\SysWOW64\drivers\")
    | where TargetFileName in~ (_wiper_drivers)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, Account = ActorUsername,
              Signal = "DriverDropped",
              Artifact = TargetFilePath,
              ParentProcessName = ActingProcessName;
union sc_create, svc_install, driver_drops
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Moses Staff DCSrv Disk-Wiper Service Installation
id: 9b6ff0c2-4d1f-4e0a-9d33-1c1a0b9f1f10
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects installation of the DCSrv / DCrypt Windows service or its kernel
  driver (dcsrv.sys / dcrypt.sys) — the signature destructive payload of
  Moses Staff (DEV-0500 / Marigold Sandstorm), a fork of DiskCryptor used
  to encrypt MBRs without a ransom demand against Israeli targets.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2021/mosesstaff-targeting-israeli-companies/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/04/07/dev-0500/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1009/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: system
detection:
  selection_svc_name:
    EventID: 7045
    ServiceName|contains:
      - 'DCSrv'
      - 'DCrypt'
  selection_svc_image:
    EventID: 7045
    ImagePath|contains:
      - 'dcsrv.sys'
      - 'dcrypt.sys'
      - 'dcrypt_setup.sys'
  condition: selection_svc_name or selection_svc_image
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise deployment of DiskCryptor for full-disk encryption (rare; validate against asset inventory)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1561.002
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1543.003
  - detection.threat_hunting
ucs.rationale: DCSrv/DCrypt is Moses Staff's bespoke wiper documented by Check Point (Nov 2021) and Microsoft (DEV-0500, 2022). Moses Staff are atypical for an Iran-aligned cluster because they masquerade destruction as ransomware but never demand payment — so the DCSrv driver and its sc.exe service-create chain is essentially pathognomonic for this actor. Generic wiper detections (vssadmin, fsutil) miss it beca

ucs.title: [LLM] Moses Staff PyDCrypt loader fan-out via PsExec / WMI to admin shares
ucs.description: Hunts the PyDCrypt loader stage — a PyInstaller-packaged Python binary Moses Staff uses on a beachhead host (typically an Exchange server they web-shelled via ProxyShell) to push the DCSrv wiper across the estate using PsExec, WMIC and SMB admin shares within a tight window. Catches the actor's lateral-deployment chain before the wiper detonates.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("python.exe","pythonw.exe","python3.exe") OR Processes.parent_process="*pydcrypt*" OR Processes.parent_process="*PyDCrypt*") AND (Processes.process_name IN ("psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","cmd.exe") AND (Processes.process="*\\\\*\\admin$*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\*\\c$*" OR Processes.process="*/node:*" OR Processes.process="*-accepteula*" OR Processes.process="*process call create*" OR Processes.process="*\\dcsrv*" OR Processes.process="*\\dcrypt*")) BY host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(host) as RemoteHostFanout values(process_name) as Tools by parent_process user | where RemoteHostFanout >= 3 OR mvcount(Tools) >= 2 | `convert_timeformat(firstTime,lastTime)` | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 600;
let _python_parents = dynamic(["python.exe","pythonw.exe","python3.exe"]);
let _admin_tools = dynamic(["psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe"]);
let PyBeachhead = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_python_parents)
        or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("PyDCrypt","pydcrypt","pyinstaller")
        or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "Python"
        or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ ("python.exe","pythonw.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project BeachheadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildBin  = FileName,
              ChildCmd  = ProcessCommandLine;
PyBeachhead
| where ChildBin in~ (_admin_tools)
| where ChildCmd has_any (@"\\admin$", @"\c$\windows", "-accepteula", "/node:", "process call create", "dcsrv", "dcrypt")
| summarize FirstSeen = min(BeachheadTime),
            LastSeen = max(BeachheadTime),
            RemoteTargets = make_set(extract(@"\\\\([A-Za-z0-9._-]+)\\", 1, ChildCmd), 50),
            Tools = make_set(ChildBin),
            Cmds  = make_set(ChildCmd, 25)
            by DeviceName, AccountName, ParentBin, ParentCmd
| extend RemoteHostFanout = array_length(RemoteTargets)
| where RemoteHostFanout >= 3 or array_length(Tools) >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let _python_parents = dynamic(["python.exe","pythonw.exe","python3.exe"]);
let _admin_tools = dynamic(["psexec.exe","psexec64.exe","paexec.exe","wmic.exe","sc.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (_python_parents) or ParentProcessName has_any ("PyDCrypt","pydcrypt")
| where ChildLeaf in (_admin_tools)
| where CommandLine has_any (@"\\admin$", @"\c$\windows", "-accepteula", "/node:", "process call create", "dcsrv", "dcrypt")
| extend RemoteHost = extract(@"\\\\([A-Za-z0-9._-]+)\\", 1, CommandLine)
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            RemoteTargets = make_set(RemoteHost, 50),
            Tools = make_set(ChildLeaf),
            Cmds  = make_set(CommandLine, 25)
            by Computer, Account, ParentProcessName
| extend RemoteHostFanout = array_length(RemoteTargets)
| where RemoteHostFanout >= 3 or array_length(Tools) >= 2
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Moses Staff is one of the very few destructive crews to ship its lateral-deployment loader (PyDCrypt) as a PyInstaller-packed Python binary — most APTs use C/C# or Cobalt Strike. Pairing python.exe/PyInstaller-tagged parents with PsExec or WMIC fan-out across admin shares, with DCSrv/DCrypt artefacts in the command line, fingerprints the rollout phase that follows their typical Exchange ProxyShell
